patients.name: Morrison, Robert J
patients.summary: 73 y.o. man with HFrEF (EF ~30%), T2DM on insulin, persistent AFib, CKD3b, CAD; longitudinal outpatient case.

patients.name: Sandoval, Maria I
patients.summary: 22 y.o. woman with 6 months of polyarthritis, alopecia, fatigue; ANA+ 1:80; uninsured. Diagnostic / management assessment case.
patients.caseTitle: Peeling and Plummeting (adapted from NEJM Clinical Problem-Solving, 2024)

patients.name: Nguyen, Lan T
patients.summary: 72 y.o. Vietnamese-speaking woman s/p prolonged ICU admission for severe gallstone pancreatitis; incidental 2.5 cm RUL lung nodule discovered HD~5; preparing for discharge HD 57. Management assessment case.
patients.caseTitle: The lung nodule that almost left (adapted from management case bank)

patients.name: Bell, Marcus D
patients.summary: 43 y.o. previously healthy man with acute unprovoked PE + extensive DVT and an incidental 17 cm liver mass (with portal-vein thrombus) concerning for malignancy; later readmitted with progressed bilateral PE after a peri-procedural anticoagulation hold. Management assessment case.
patients.caseTitle: The PE and the liver mass (adapted from management case bank)

patients.name: Whitlock, Sarah L
patients.summary: 50 y.o. woman with decompensated alcohol-related cirrhosis (hepatic encephalopathy, diuretic-refractory ascites, right hepatic hydrothorax) referred for therapeutic drainage; harmed by prophylactic peri-procedural FFP (flash pulmonary edema/ICU); later transplanted. Management assessment case.
patients.caseTitle: The prophylactic FFP that drowned her (adapted from management case bank)

patients.name: Pierce, Dorothy A
patients.summary: 72 y.o. woman with poorly-controlled HTN and T2DM; new-onset perioperative atrial fibrillation after urgent open cholecystectomy for perforated cholecystitis (brief runs POD#1, 2-hour asymptomatic episode POD#4). Management assessment case.
patients.caseTitle: The post-op atrial fibrillation (adapted from management case bank)

patients.name: Brooks, Janet M
patients.summary: 55 y.o. woman with alcohol-related cirrhosis and involuntary weight loss; variceal bleed → emergent TIPS → prolonged fevers/refractory shock with a negative infectious workup, nearly transitioned to comfort measures before recovering once antibiotics were stopped (drug fever). Management assessment case.
patients.caseTitle: The drug fever that almost killed her (adapted from management case bank)
